---
title: "Teaching"
toc: true
link-external-newwindow: true
format:
  html:
    include-after-body:
      text: |
        <span id="particles-js"></span>
        <script src="js/particles.js"></script>
        <script src="js/app.js"></script>
---

<style>
main.content {
  background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.88);
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 1.2rem 1.6rem;
  backdrop-filter: blur(3px);
  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(3px);
  pointer-events: none;
}
main.content a,
main.content button,
main.content details,
main.content summary {
  pointer-events: auto;
}
body.quarto-dark main.content {
  background: rgba(34, 34, 34, 0.88);
}
#quarto-margin-sidebar {
  pointer-events: none;
}
#quarto-margin-sidebar a {
  pointer-events: auto;
}
</style>

## Teaching Statement

My teaching focuses on helping students develop a scientific approach to politics across all course levels. This involves three key elements: First, I emphasize scientific literacy by distinguishing social science from journalistic and rhetorical approaches, showing how we seek systematic patterns rather than explaining single events or advocating positions. Second, I demonstrate the value of scientific methods both for deepening understanding and informing effective public policy. Finally, I guide students in conducting their own research, both individually and collaboratively, transforming them from consumers to creators of political knowledge.
Through interactive learning experiences, hands-on research projects, and real-world applications, I help students build analytical skills that serve them beyond the classroom while maintaining an inclusive environment where students from all backgrounds can engage meaningfully with quantitative methods.

## List of Courses

##### At <a href="https://www.uidaho.edu" target="_blank"> **the University of Idaho** </a> (as a Postdoctoral Fellow)

- **POLS 4740 Public Opinion and Political Behavior** (Undergraduate) <a href="files/syllabi/pols4740.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/pols4740.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Ghost Syllabus Component <a href="files/syllabi/pols4740-full.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/pols4740-full.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Fall 2025

##### At <a href="https://sgpp.arizona.edu" target="_blank"> **the University of Arizona** </a> (as a Research Social Scientist)

- **POL/SOC 297B/397B The Origins of Data in Politics and Policy** (Undergraduate) <a href="files/syllabi/pol397b.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/pol397b.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Spring 2024, Spring 2025

##### At <a href="https://iibf.yeditepe.edu.tr/en/political-science-and-international-relations-department-en" target="_blank"> **Yeditepe University** </a> (as an Assistant Professor)

- **POLS 624 Quantitative Methods and Statistics** (Graduate) <a href="files/syllabi/pols624.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/pols624.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Spring 2023

- **PSIR 480 Applied Research in Political Science** (Undergraduate) <a href="files/syllabi/psir480.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/psir480.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Spring 2023

- **RSCH 410 Scientific Research Methods** (Undergraduate; Service-course) <a href="files/syllabi/rsch410.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/rsch410.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Fall 2022, Spring 2023

- **PSIR 101 Introduction to Political Science** (Undergraduate) <a href="files/syllabi/psir101.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/psir101.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Fall 2022, Spring 2023

##### At <a href="https://www.bilgi.edu.tr/en/academic/faculty-of-social-sciences-and-humanities/international-relations/" target="_blank"> **Istanbul Bilgi University** </a> (as an Adjunct Lecturer)

- **IR 408 Elections and Party Politics** (Undergraduate) <a href="files/syllabi/ir408.pdf" target="_blank">{{< iconify bi eye >}} View</a> <a href="files/syllabi/ir408.pdf" download>{{< iconify bi download >}} Download</a>
    - Fall 2021

<!-- Google tag (gtag.js) -->
<script async src="https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=G-C9REN6Q65E"></script>
<script>
  window.dataLayer = window.dataLayer || [];
  function gtag(){dataLayer.push(arguments);}
  gtag('js', new Date());

  gtag('config', 'G-C9REN6Q65E');
</script>
